questions.q: In which Indian state is Jhansi Fort located?
questions.explanation: Jhansi Fort stands in the city of Jhansi in the Bundelkhand region of Uttar Pradesh.

questions.q: Jhansi Fort is most famously associated with which queen?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai, the queen of Jhansi, defended the fort during the 1857 uprising against the British.

questions.q: During which major historical event did Rani Lakshmibai defend Jhansi Fort?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai defended Jhansi Fort during the Revolt of 1857, India's first major uprising against British rule.

questions.q: On what type of terrain is Jhansi Fort built?
questions.explanation: Jhansi Fort is a hilltop fortress built on a rocky hill (Bangira) overlooking the city.

questions.q: Jhansi Fort lies in which historical region of central India?
questions.explanation: Jhansi is part of the Bundelkhand region spanning parts of Uttar Pradesh and Madhya Pradesh.

questions.q: Which empire annexed Jhansi using the Doctrine of Lapse, sparking the queen's resistance?
questions.explanation: The British East India Company annexed Jhansi in 1853 under the Doctrine of Lapse after the raja died without a natural heir.

questions.q: What is the primary building material of Jhansi Fort's massive walls?
questions.explanation: The fort's thick ramparts were built mainly of granite and stone to withstand cannon fire and sieges.

questions.q: Rani Lakshmibai is celebrated in India primarily as a symbol of what?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai is honoured as a heroine of India's freedom struggle and a symbol of valour and resistance.

questions.q: What kind of structure is Jhansi Fort?
questions.explanation: Jhansi Fort is a fortified military stronghold built for defence, with thick walls, gates and bastions.

questions.q: Which dynasty originally built Jhansi Fort in the 17th century?
questions.explanation: Jhansi Fort was built by the Bundela Rajput rulers, who dominated the Bundelkhand region.

questions.q: Which Bundela ruler is credited with building Jhansi Fort in 1613?
options: Bir Singh Deo (Bir Singh Ju Deo)
questions.explanation: Raja Bir Singh Deo (Bir Singh Ju Deo) of the Orchha-based Bundela dynasty built Jhansi Fort around 1613.

questions.q: On which hill in Jhansi was the fort constructed?
questions.explanation: Jhansi Fort was built on Bangira, a large rocky hill that dominates the surrounding plains.

questions.q: What was Rani Lakshmibai's birth name before she became queen of Jhansi?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai was born Manikarnika, called Manu, and took the name Lakshmibai after marrying the Raja of Jhansi.

questions.q: British policy used to annex Jhansi after the raja's death was known as the:
questions.explanation: Lord Dalhousie's Doctrine of Lapse refused to recognise the adopted heir, leading to the annexation of Jhansi in 1853.

questions.q: Who was Rani Lakshmibai's husband, the ruler of Jhansi?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai was married to Maharaja Gangadhar Rao Newalkar, the Raja of Jhansi, who died in 1853.

questions.q: Which famous phrase is attributed to Rani Lakshmibai in defiance of the British?
options: "Main apni Jhansi nahi doongi" (I will not give up my Jhansi)
questions.explanation: The defiant words "Main apni Jhansi nahi doongi" are popularly attributed to Rani Lakshmibai's resistance against British annexation.

questions.q: British forces under which commander besieged Jhansi Fort in March 1858?
questions.explanation: General Sir Hugh Rose led the British siege of Jhansi in March 1858 during the campaign to crush the revolt.

questions.q: After fleeing Jhansi, Rani Lakshmibai died fighting near which city in June 1858?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai died in battle near Gwalior in June 1858 while resisting the British advance.

questions.q: A loyal woman soldier who is said to have disguised herself as the queen to help her escape Jhansi was:
questions.explanation: Jhalkari Bai, a soldier in the queen's army, is celebrated for impersonating Rani Lakshmibai to aid her escape from the fort.

questions.q: Within Jhansi Fort, a temple dedicated to which deity is associated with the queen's worship?
questions.explanation: The fort contains shrines including one linked to the goddess Lakshmi and a Ganesh temple where the queen is said to have prayed.

questions.q: The dramatic feat attributed to Rani Lakshmibai during her escape from Jhansi Fort was that she:
options: Leapt from the fort walls on horseback with her adopted son tied to her back
Tunnelled out through a secret passage
Disguised herself as a British officer
questions.explanation: Legend holds that Rani Lakshmibai leapt from the fort ramparts on her horse with her young adopted son bound to her back to escape the siege.

questions.q: How many gates does Jhansi Fort traditionally have for access?
questions.explanation: Jhansi Fort is traditionally said to have ten gates, several of which still survive, such as Khanderao Gate and Datia Darwaza.

questions.q: What is the name of the large cannon traditionally associated with Jhansi Fort's defence?
questions.explanation: Kadak Bijli is the famous cannon said to have been used in the defence of Jhansi Fort during the 1857 revolt.

questions.q: After the Bundelas, which power controlled Jhansi before it passed effectively under the East India Company?
questions.explanation: Jhansi came under Maratha control in the 18th century, with the Newalkar family governing as subedars before the Company's involvement.

questions.q: Approximately in which decade did Jhansi rise as a Maratha administrative centre under a subedar?
questions.explanation: In the early-to-mid 18th century (around the 1730s onward) the Marathas established Jhansi as a base, appointing a subedar to govern Bundelkhand.

questions.q: What is the name of the adopted son in whose name Rani Lakshmibai claimed the throne of Jhansi?
questions.explanation: Rani Lakshmibai sought recognition for her adopted son Damodar Rao as heir, which the British refused under the Doctrine of Lapse.

questions.q: Which leader joined forces with Rani Lakshmibai and captured Gwalior fort with her in 1858?
questions.explanation: The Maratha general Tatya Tope allied with Rani Lakshmibai, and together they seized Gwalior shortly before her final battle.

questions.q: The Hindi poem immortalising Rani Lakshmibai with the line 'Khoob ladi mardani woh to Jhansi wali rani thi' was written by:
questions.explanation: Subhadra Kumari Chauhan wrote the celebrated poem 'Jhansi Ki Rani', whose refrain praises the queen of Jhansi who fought valiantly.

questions.q: Within the fort grounds stands a memorial board / structure marking events of the revolt; the fort today is maintained by which body?
questions.explanation: Jhansi Fort is a protected monument under the care of the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).

questions.q: Roughly how high is the rocky hill on which Jhansi Fort sits above the surrounding city?
options: Several metres only, barely a mound
Tens of metres, a prominent rocky elevation
Over 1,000 metres like a mountain peak
Below the level of the plain
questions.explanation: The fort crowns Bangira hill, a prominent rocky elevation rising tens of metres above the city, giving it a commanding defensive position.
